rationale: The cited paper appears only as part of a grouped citation in the contributions section when the authors note broader work on political identity and consumer evaluations. The citing paper does not use Han et al. (2019) to build hypotheses, define mechanisms, choose measures, or interpret core results in a paper-specific way. Its political-segmentation logic is primarily grounded in other sources on work-life conflict, so this citation functions as background positioning rather than a meaningful building block.
